passages.text: Glass is one of the few materials that can be recycled endlessly without loss of quality. ( ① ) When glass products are collected and sent to the recycling plant, they are crushed into small pieces called cullet. ( ② ) The cullet is then mixed with raw materials like sand and soda ash and melted in a furnace. ( ③ ) The resulting mixture can be molded into new bottles and jars identical to the originals. ( ④ ) In fact, using cullet reduces the energy required to melt the batch because it melts at a lower temperature. ( ⑤ ) For this reason, recycling glass not only conserves resources but also cuts greenhouse-gas emissions.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? This process saves about 30 percent of the energy needed to make glass from virgin materials.
questions.explanation: ④에서 파유리(cullet)가 더 낮은 온도에서 녹아 에너지를 절약한다고 설명. 주어진 문장의 'This process'는 이 에너지 절약 과정을 가리키며, ⑤의 'For this reason'과 연결되어 전체적인 결론을 이끌어냄.

passages.text: Most adults need between seven and nine hours of sleep each night, yet surveys show that many people fall short of this target. ( ① ) Chronic lack of sleep has been linked to a higher risk of heart disease, obesity, and depression. ( ② ) Laboratory studies also reveal that reaction times slow dramatically after extended wakefulness. ( ③ ) Drivers who have been awake for 20 hours perform about as poorly as those with a blood-alcohol level of 0.08 percent. ( ④ ) Consequently, drowsy driving is responsible for thousands of accidents each year. ( ⑤ ) Getting consistent, high-quality rest is therefore one of the simplest ways to protect both physical and mental health.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Even a single night of poor sleep can impair memory and judgment the following day.
questions.explanation: 앞 문장의 a higher risk of heart disease, obesity, and depression 부분의 부정적인 결과들이 Even a single night 으로 이어지며 부정적 결과가 재언급 됨.

passages.text: Coral reefs owe their brilliant colors to symbiotic algae that live inside coral tissue. ( ① ) When ocean temperatures rise, corals become stressed and expel these algae. ( ② ) Without their colorful partners, the corals turn ghostly white, a phenomenon known as bleaching. ( ③ ) Bleached corals are weaker and more susceptible to disease. ( ④ ) Large-scale bleaching events have become more frequent over the past three decades, threatening reef ecosystems worldwide. ( ⑤ ) Scientists warn that limiting global warming is essential to preserve these biodiverse habitats.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? If temperatures remain elevated, the algae may never return, causing the coral to die.
questions.explanation: ③에서 산호가 약해지고 질병에 취약해진다고 설명. 주어진 문장은 '온도가 계속 높으면'이라는 조건 하에 산호가 죽는 최악의 결과를 제시. 이는 ④ 뒤의 '대규모 백화 현상'의 직접적 원인이 됨.

passages.text: Battery technology has improved rapidly in recent years. ( ① ) Modern lithium-ion packs store more energy while weighing less than their predecessors. ( ② ) As ranges climb above 500 kilometers per charge, so-called range anxiety begins to fade. ( ③ ) Governments are also expanding charging-station networks along major highways. ( ④ ) In addition, many countries now offer tax credits or rebates to purchasers of electric vehicles. ( ⑤ ) Analysts predict that electric cars could account for half of all new car sales by 2030.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? As a result, more drivers feel confident making the switch to electric models.
questions.explanation: ①~④는 전기차로의 전환을 유도하는 긍정적 요인들(기술 발전, 주행거리 불안 해소, 충전소 확대, 보조금)을 나열함. 주어진 문장은 이 모든 요인들의 '결과'로 운전자들이 자신감을 얻는다는 내용이므로, 모든 요인이 언급된 후이자 최종 전망 전인 ⑤가 가장 적절.

passages.text: In the late nineteenth century, a group of young painters broke away from the rigid rules of France's art academies. ( ① ) They began painting outdoors to capture changing light and atmosphere. ( ② ) Critics at the time dismissed their loose brushstrokes and bright colors as unfinished and childish. ( ③ ) Nevertheless, the public slowly warmed to their fresh perspective. ( ④ ) Today works by Monet, Renoir, and Degas are celebrated worldwide. ( ⑤ ) Their movement forever altered the course of modern art.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Instead of striving for photographic realism, they sought to convey the fleeting impressions of a moment.
questions.explanation: 1번 문장 앞의 이전의 학파를 부정한다는 내용이 나옴. 이걸 이어서 주어진 문장에 기존의 형태를 부수고 새로운 자기들만의 방식을 찾는다는 내용이 주어진 문장 뒷부분과 1번뒷 문장으로 이어져야 자연스러움.

passages.text: Plastic trash does not disappear once it enters the ocean. ( ① ) Waves and sunlight gradually break larger items into tiny fragments called microplastics. ( ② ) These fragments can travel vast distances, turning up on remote Arctic beaches and deep-sea trenches alike. ( ③ ) Studies have detected microplastics in seafood, table salt, and even drinking water. ( ④ ) Because plastics attract toxic chemicals, they may concentrate pollutants as they move through the food chain. ( ⑤ ) Reducing single-use plastics on land is therefore critical for protecting marine life.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? These particles are small enough to be ingested by plankton at the very base of the oceanic food web.
questions.explanation: ②에서 미세플라스틱의 광범위한 확산을 언급. 주어진 문장은 'These particles'를 받아 먹이사슬의 최하위인 플랑크톤에게 섭취될 만큼 작다고 설명. 이는 ③ 뒤의 '해산물, 소금 등에서 발견'된다는 내용의 시작점이 됨.

passages.text: Centuries ago, trade in China grew so brisk that bulky metal coins became impractical. ( ① ) Merchants began leaving their heavy currency with trusted agents and receiving paper receipts instead. ( ② ) Over time, these receipts started circulating hand-to-hand as payment for goods. ( ③ ) The government soon recognized the convenience of this system and issued officially sanctioned notes. ( ④ ) Counterfeiting remained a persistent challenge, prompting the use of special dyes and intricate designs. ( ⑤ ) The innovation later spread along the Silk Road, influencing monetary systems across Eurasia.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? This practice laid the groundwork for what we now call paper money.
questions.explanation: ①에서 상인들이 '종이 영수증'을 받기 시작했다고 설명. 주어진 문장의 'This practice'가 이 행위를 가리키며, 이것이 '지폐의 기원'이 되었다고 설명. ② 뒤에서 'these receipts'가 화폐처럼 유통되기 시작했다는 내용으로 이어짐.

passages.text: Sharing personal information online has never been easier—or riskier. ( ① ) Social-media platforms generate revenue by collecting user data and selling targeted advertisements. ( ② ) Meanwhile, data breaches at major corporations have exposed millions of customers' records. ( ③ ) As public concern mounts, several governments have enacted strict privacy laws requiring companies to obtain explicit consent before gathering data. ( ④ ) Critics argue that enforcement remains uneven and penalties too lenient. ( ⑤ ) Ultimately, users must stay vigilant and adjust privacy settings to safeguard their digital identities.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? In theory, these regulations give individuals more control over how their information is used.
questions.explanation: ③에서 정부가 개인정보 보호법을 제정했다는 사실을 언급. 주어진 문장은 'these regulations'를 받아 그 법의 이론적 목적을 설명함. ④ 뒤의 'Critics argue'는 이 이론과 현실의 차이를 지적하며 반론을 제기함.

passages.text: Wildfires play a natural role in many forest ecosystems. ( ① ) Low-intensity burns clear away dead vegetation, making room for new growth. ( ② ) Decades of aggressive fire suppression, however, have allowed flammable material to accumulate. ( ③ ) As a result, today's fires often burn hotter and spread faster than historical ones. ( ④ ) Fire ecologists now advocate selective thinning and controlled burns to restore healthier conditions. ( ⑤ ) Communities located near forests must adapt to increased smoke and the potential for sudden evacuations.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Climate change further complicates the picture by lengthening fire seasons and drying out soils.
questions.explanation: ③에서 산불이 더 뜨거워지고 빨리 퍼지는 원인을 설명. 주어진 문장은 '기후 변화'가 이 상황을 '더욱 복잡하게' 만든다고 추가적인 악화 요인을 제시. 이는 ④ 뒤의 해결책 모색으로 자연스럽게 연결됨.

passages.text: Volunteering delivers benefits that extend beyond the recipients of charitable work. ( ① ) Studies show that people who volunteer regularly report higher life satisfaction. ( ② ) The act of giving time fosters a sense of purpose and social connection. ( ③ ) It can even lower stress by shifting attention away from personal worries. ( ④ ) Retirement counselors therefore encourage seniors to find volunteer roles that match their interests and skills. ( ⑤ ) Simple acts of service can create ripple effects that strengthen entire communities.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Psychologists also note improvements in mental health, including reduced symptoms of anxiety and depression.
questions.explanation: ①, ②, ③은 자원봉사의 긍정적 효과(삶의 만족도, 목적의식, 스트레스 감소)를 나열함. 주어진 문장은 'also'를 사용해 '정신 건강 개선'이라는 추가적인 이점을 제시하므로, 모든 이점이 언급된 후이자 결론적 조언 전인 ④가 적절.

passages.text: Antibiotic-resistant bacteria are spreading rapidly worldwide. ( ① ) Hospitals report rising cases where conventional drugs no longer work. ( ② ) Public-health officials warn that routine surgeries could become high-risk procedures. ( ③ ) Efforts to curb overuse of antibiotics in livestock and improve stewardship in clinics are under way. ( ④ ) Researchers are also racing to develop novel treatments, but progress remains slow. ( ⑤ ) The situation demands a multi-pronged approach involving governments, doctors, and the public.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Without decisive global action, even minor infections could once again become deadly.
questions.explanation: ②까지 항생제 내성의 심각한 상황을 경고. 주어진 문장은 '결정적 조치 없이는' 사소한 감염도 치명적일 수 있다는 강력한 경고로, ③ 뒤의 대응 노력(curb overuse)의 당위성을 부여함.

passages.text: Mindfulness meditation is becoming a popular tool for managing workplace stress. ( ① ) Studies show regular practice enhances focus and emotional regulation. ( ② ) Corporate wellness programs now include short guided sessions before meetings. ( ③ ) Experts caution, however, that sustained benefits require consistent practice over months. ( ④ ) Even so, many employees report feeling calmer and more productive. ( ⑤ ) The key is consistency, not duration.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? In fact, a brief daily session can significantly lower stress hormone levels.
questions.explanation: ②에서 기업들이 짧은 명상 세션을 도입한다고 설명. 주어진 문장은 'In fact'를 통해 이 짧은 세션의 구체적인 생리학적 효과(스트레스 호르몬 감소)를 부연 설명함. ③ 뒤의 'however'는 이 단기 효과와 대조되는 장기적 효과를 언급.

passages.text: Thanks to 3-D printing, manufacturers can move from design to prototype in a single afternoon. ( ① ) Traditional tooling often takes weeks and significant capital investment. ( ② ) By contrast, a digital file can be sent directly to a printer on the factory floor. ( ③ ) As a result, smaller firms can compete with larger rivals by iterating products at lightning speed. ( ④ ) Analysts predict the technology will reshape supply chains over the next decade. ( ⑤ ) The shift from mass production to mass customization is already under way.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? This capability allows factories to pivot quickly when customer demand shifts unexpectedly.
questions.explanation: ②에서 디지털 파일을 프린터로 직접 보낼 수 있다는 장점을 설명. 주어진 문장의 'This capability'가 이를 가리키며, 그 덕분에 수요 변화에 '빨리 대응'할 수 있다는 결과를 설명. 이는 ③ 뒤의 소규모 기업 경쟁력 강화로 이어짐.

passages.text: Music therapy is gaining traction as a complementary treatment for dementia. ( ① ) Rhythms and melodies engage brain regions involved in memory and emotion. ( ② ) Sessions often involve singing familiar songs or tapping to a beat. ( ③ ) Therapists use these moments to stimulate conversation and foster social connection. ( ④ ) Early evidence suggests this approach can reduce agitation and improve quality of life. ( ⑤ ) The goal is not to cure the disease, but to enhance well-being.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Remarkably, patients who struggle to recall loved ones' names can still sing entire songs from their youth.
questions.explanation: ②에서 익숙한 노래를 부르는 세션을 설명. 주어진 문장은 'Remarkably'를 통해 이 세션에서 나타나는 놀라운 현상(기억상실 환자가 노래를 부름)을 구체적으로 묘사함. ③ 뒤의 'these moments'가 바로 이 현상을 가리킴.

passages.text: Delivering aid to remote disaster zones remains a logistical nightmare. ( ① ) Roads are frequently blocked and power outages disable communication. ( ② ) Drones equipped with GPS are emerging as a solution. ( ③ ) Pilot programs in Rwanda have already delivered blood to rural clinics via autonomous aircraft. ( ④ ) International agencies are now exploring scale-up to other regions. ( ⑤ ) The technology promises to revolutionize humanitarian response.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? As a result, life-saving supplies can arrive within hours instead of days.
questions.explanation: ②에서 드론이 해결책으로 등장. 주어진 문장은 그 '결과'로 구호품 도착 시간이 단축된다고 설명. ③ 뒤의 르완다 사례는 이 결과에 대한 구체적인 증거가 됨.

passages.text: Modern astronomy generates more images than professionals can analyze. ( ① ) Online platforms invite ordinary citizens to classify galaxies or spot exoplanet transits. ( ② ) Their contributions have led to several unexpected discoveries, including a new type of star cluster. ( ③ ) Participants also gain firsthand experience with scientific methodology. ( ④ ) The model demonstrates how crowd-sourced science can accelerate progress. ( ⑤ ) This approach is now being applied to fields like biology and climate science.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Collectively, these volunteers can sift through data far faster than professional researchers alone.
questions.explanation: ①에서 시민 과학 플랫폼을 소개. 주어진 문장은 'these volunteers'가 전문가보다 데이터를 더 빨리 처리할 수 있다는 핵심 장점을 설명. ② 뒤의 'Their contributions'가 이 빠른 처리 덕분에 가능해진 발견들을 언급.

passages.text: The universe seems to contain five times more dark matter than ordinary matter. ( ① ) Astronomers infer its presence from the way galaxies rotate and light bends. ( ② ) Sensitive detectors are buried deep underground to avoid cosmic radiation. ( ③ ) Some theorists propose that dark matter may interact only through gravity. ( ④ ) Future missions in space hope to gather new clues. ( ⑤ ) The mystery remains one of the biggest puzzles in physics.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Yet despite decades of searching, no experiment has conclusively detected a single particle.
questions.explanation: ②에서 암흑물질 탐지기 설치를 언급. 주어진 문장은 'Yet'을 통해 수십 년간의 탐색 노력에도 불구하고 '아무것도 탐지하지 못했다'는 반전 결과를 제시. 이는 ③ 뒤의 새로운 이론 제시로 이어짐.

passages.text: Cities are experimenting with strategies to combat the urban heat-island effect. ( ① ) Cool roofs coated with reflective paint bounce sunlight back into the atmosphere. ( ② ) Replacing asphalt with permeable, light-colored pavements also helps. ( ③ ) Community groups advocate for expanding green spaces along streets. ( ④ ) Such measures not only reduce cooling costs but also improve air quality. ( ⑤ ) These initiatives show how urban design can mitigate climate change impacts.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Planting even two trees per house can lower neighborhood temperatures by several degrees.
questions.explanation: ③에서 녹지 공간 확장을 주장. 주어진 문장은 나무 심기의 구체적인 온도 저감 효과를 제시. ④ 뒤의 'Such measures'는 앞서 언급된 지붕, 포장, 나무 심기 등을 모두 포함하는 요약.

passages.text: Smartphone apps now connect consumers with restaurants that have surplus food nearing closing time. ( ① ) Users can purchase unsold meals at steep discounts, saving money while reducing waste. ( ② ) Participating eateries benefit by recovering some costs that would otherwise be lost. ( ③ ) Environmental advocates applaud the model for its simplicity. ( ④ ) Developers plan to expand the service to include supermarkets and bakeries next. ( ⑤ ) This shows the power of technology to address social problems.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? In just one year, participating restaurants diverted over two million meals from landfills.
questions.explanation: ③까지 앱 모델의 장점을 설명. 주어진 문장은 구체적인 성과(2백만 끼니 절약)를 제시하며 앞 내용들을 뒷받침. ④ 뒤의 'Developers plan to expand'는 이 성공에 기반한 향후 계획을 설명.

passages.text: Scientists have engineered enzymes that break down PET plastic in a matter of hours. ( ① ) The discovery emerged from studying microbes living in a recycling plant. ( ② ) By tweaking the enzyme’s structure, researchers dramatically sped up the reaction. ( ③ ) Pilot facilities are now testing the process on shredded beverage bottles. ( ④ ) If successful, it could shift the economics of plastic reuse worldwide. ( ⑤ ) This breakthrough offers a potential solution to a major environmental problem.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Scaled up, the technique could recycle billions of bottles currently deemed unrecoverable.
questions.explanation: ③에서 파일럿 시설 테스트를 언급. 주어진 문장은 이 기술이 '대규모로 확장될 경우'의 잠재력을 설명. ④ 뒤의 'If successful'은 이 잠재력이 실현될 경우의 경제적 파급 효과를 설명.

passages.text: Archaeologists studying the Nazca culture in Peru have uncovered an extensive system of underground aqueducts. ( ① ) Called puquios, the spiraling shafts drew groundwater to the surface through simple gravity. ( ② ) They were lined with stones to prevent collapse and capped with vents that eased maintenance. ( ③ ) The ingenuity of the design kept water flowing even during prolonged droughts. ( ④ ) Without such engineering, large-scale settlement in the desert plain would have been impossible. ( ⑤ ) Today several of the aqueducts still function, supplying nearby villages.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? These channels allowed farmers to harvest two crops a year in an otherwise arid climate.
questions.explanation: ③에서 수로의 독창적 설계를 칭찬. 주어진 문장은 'These channels'를 받아 그 수로 덕분에 가능해진 농업적 성과(연 2회 수확)를 설명. 이는 ④ 뒤의 'such engineering'이 없었다면 정착이 불가능했다는 결론으로 이어짐.

passages.text: Job seekers often rehearse answers to common interview questions, but they sometimes overlook body language. ( ① ) Recruiters say posture, gestures, and facial expressions can speak louder than any résumé bullet. ( ② ) Fidgeting or crossing arms, by contrast, may signal discomfort or defensiveness. ( ③ ) Because first impressions form within seconds, nonverbal cues deserve equal preparation time. ( ④ ) Practicing in front of a mirror or recording oneself can help identify habits that need adjustment. ( ⑤ ) Mastering these silent signals can tip the balance when candidates have similar qualifications.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Keeping steady eye contact, for instance, shows confidence without a single word.
questions.explanation: ①에서 비언어적 신호의 중요성을 언급. 주어진 문장은 그 예시로 '눈맞춤'을 제시. ② 뒤의 'by contrast'는 이 긍정적 예시와 대조되는 부정적 예시(꼼지락거림)를 제시.

passages.text: Electrolyzers split water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity. ( ① ) When operated during periods of excess solar or wind power, they help stabilize the grid. ( ② ) Storing the hydrogen effectively turns clean electricity into a transportable fuel. ( ③ ) Researchers are now developing catalytic membranes that slash production costs. ( ④ ) Countries like Germany plan to build “hydrogen valleys” where industry, heating, and transport all run on the fuel. ( ⑤ ) Critics note, however, that large-scale deployment requires vast amounts of freshwater.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? The resulting gas can then be fed directly into existing natural-gas pipelines.
questions.explanation: ②에서 수소를 저장하여 운송 가능한 연료로 만든다고 설명. 주어진 문장은 이 '가스'를 기존 파이프라인에 직접 공급할 수 있다는 활용 방안을 제시. ③ 뒤의 연구 개발 내용과 자연스럽게 연결됨.

passages.text: Economists use the term sunk cost fallacy to describe irrational decision-making. ( ① ) Imagine buying an expensive concert ticket and then falling ill on the day of the show. ( ② ) Rationally, staying home would maximize comfort without changing the fact that the ticket is paid for. ( ③ ) Many concertgoers still drag themselves to the venue despite feeling miserable. ( ④ ) Psychologists suggest that admitting a loss feels like accepting failure, triggering defensive behavior. ( ⑤ ) Recognizing this bias is the first step toward making objectively better choices.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Yet people often keep pouring time or money into a doomed endeavor simply because they have already invested so much.
questions.explanation: ③에서 비합리적인 행동(아픈데 콘서트 감)의 예시를 제시. 주어진 문장은 'Yet'을 통해 이러한 비합리적 행동의 일반적인 경향을 설명. ④ 뒤의 내용은 이 행동의 심리학적 원인을 분석.

passages.text: Wild bee populations are declining across several continents. ( ① ) Habitat loss, disease, and climate change all contribute to the trend. ( ② ) A growing body of evidence also implicates a class of pesticides called neonicotinoids. ( ③ ) Laboratory tests show these chemicals impair bees’ navigation and reproduction. ( ④ ) Several countries have restricted their use pending further research. ( ⑤ ) Farmers are experimenting with flowering buffer strips to provide alternative forage near fields.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? If pollinator numbers continue to drop, yields of almonds, apples, and many other crops could plummet.
questions.explanation: ③에서 살충제가 벌의 번식에 미치는 해로운 영향을 설명. 주어진 문장은 이로 인해 '수분 매개자 수가 감소하면' 농작물 수확량이 급감할 수 있다는 결과를 경고. ④ 뒤의 규제 조치는 이러한 경고에 대한 대응.

passages.text: The shipping industry is eyeing autonomous cargo ships as a way to cut costs and emissions. ( ① ) Crews account for a significant portion of operating expenses on long voyages. ( ② ) Remote monitoring centers on shore would take over many navigation tasks. ( ③ ) Machine-learning algorithms continuously update routes to avoid storms and congested lanes. ( ④ ) Regulatory agencies still need to draft safety standards before pilot projects can proceed. ( ⑤ ) Analysts predict commercial deployment before the end of the decade.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Advanced radar and lidar arrays enable the vessels to detect obstacles even in dense fog.
questions.explanation: ③까지 자율 운항 선박의 기술적 측면을 설명. 주어진 문장은 'Advanced radar and lidar'가 어떻게 악천후 속에서도 장애물을 탐지하는지 구체적인 기술을 추가 설명. 이는 ④ 뒤의 규제 논의와 연결됨.

passages.text: Debate persists over whether William Shakespeare wrote all the works attributed to him. ( ① ) Skeptics argue that a man of modest education could not produce such erudite texts. ( ② ) Others propose that nobles like Edward de Vere may have used Shakespeare as a front. ( ③ ) Mainstream scholars counter that surviving records align with the Stratford man’s authorship. ( ④ ) Regardless of the debate, the plays continue to captivate audiences worldwide. ( ⑤ ) Literary puzzles like this remind us how little survives of any writer’s personal life.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Supporters of the theory point to inconsistencies in spelling and vocabulary across the plays.
questions.explanation: ②에서 다른 작가 대필설을 제기. 주어진 문장은 'the theory'를 지지하는 근거로 '철자법과 어휘의 불일치'를 제시. ③ 뒤의 주류 학자들의 반박과 자연스럽게 대립 구도를 형성.

passages.text: Microfinance institutions provide tiny loans to people who lack collateral. ( ① ) Interest rates are higher than traditional bank loans but still lower than informal lenders. ( ② ) Group liability helps ensure repayment because peers monitor one another. ( ③ ) Longitudinal studies show that access to credit increases women’s decision-making power at home. ( ④ ) Critics caution that debt can become burdensome if profits fail to materialize. ( ⑤ ) Many NGOs now pair loans with basic business-skills training.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Borrowers typically invest in livestock or start small shops, boosting household income.
questions.explanation: ②에서 그룹 책임 제도를 설명. 주어진 문장은 대출자들이 대출금으로 '무엇을 하는지' 구체적인 용도를 설명. 이는 ③ 뒤의 여성 권한 증가라는 사회적 효과로 이어짐.

passages.text: A space elevator would rely on an ultra-long cable stretching from Earth’s surface to geostationary orbit. ( ① ) Freight climbers could ascend the ribbon using electric motors powered from the ground. ( ② ) Atmospheric lightning, micrometeoroids, and orbital debris pose significant hazards to the structure. ( ③ ) Economists argue that once built, the elevator could slash the cost of launching satellites. ( ④ ) Despite decades of conceptual work, materials science remains the primary obstacle. ( ⑤ ) Several teams are racing to produce fibers with the necessary strength-to-weight ratio.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? To overcome tensile stresses, engineers propose using a carbon-nanotube composite far stronger than steel.
questions.explanation: ④에서 재료 과학이 주된 장애물임을 언급. 주어진 문장은 이 장애물, 즉 '인장 응력'을 극복하기 위한 구체적인 해결책(탄소나노튜브)을 제시. ⑤ 뒤의 내용은 이 해결책을 개발하려는 경쟁을 설명.

passages.text: The term digital nomad describes a growing segment of the workforce. ( ① ) Rising broadband availability and relaxed visa policies make it easier to work from Bali one month and Berlin the next. ( ② ) Coworking spaces cater to the community by offering short-term memberships and networking events. ( ③ ) Critics contend that the lifestyle can blur boundaries between labor and leisure. ( ④ ) Host cities, meanwhile, debate whether the influx stimulates the local economy or drives up rents. ( ⑤ ) Some countries now issue special visas that require proof of remote income.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? These itinerant professionals combine work and travel, relying on laptops and a steady internet connection.
questions.explanation: 글의 첫 문장에서 '디지털 노마드'를 정의. 주어진 문장은 'These itinerant professionals'를 받아 그들의 특징(노트북, 인터넷 의존)을 설명하며 정의를 보충. ① 뒤에서 이들의 생활 방식이 가능해진 배경을 설명.

passages.text: Berries owe their vivid colors to compounds known as anthocyanins. ( ① ) These antioxidants neutralize free radicals linked to aging and disease. ( ② ) Clinical trials indicate that regular consumption improves blood vessel function. ( ③ ) Blueberries, strawberries, and blackcurrants each supply a unique blend of phytochemicals. ( ④ ) Freezing the fruit shortly after harvest preserves most nutrients. ( ⑤ ) Processed jams, however, often contain added sugar that offsets potential health gains.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Nutritionists recommend eating a handful of mixed berries three times a week.
questions.explanation: ②에서 베리류의 건강상 이점을 설명. 주어진 문장은 이를 바탕으로 구체적인 섭취 권장량을 제시. ③ 뒤의 내용은 다양한 베리류의 성분을 설명하며 앞선 내용을 뒷받침.

passages.text: Virtual classrooms kept education running during the pandemic. ( ① ) Students appreciated the flexibility of logging in from home. ( ② ) Yet many reported feelings of isolation and screen fatigue. ( ③ ) Technical glitches such as poor audio can derail discussion. ( ④ ) Hybrid models that blend online lectures with periodic in-person sessions aim to capture the best of both worlds. ( ⑤ ) Universities are now redesigning curricula to suit multiple delivery modes.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Teachers also lament the difficulty of reading body language through tiny webcam windows.
questions.explanation: ③에서 기술적 결함을 언급. 주어진 문장은 'also'를 사용해 교사들이 겪는 또 다른 어려움(몸짓 읽기)을 추가. ④ 뒤의 하이브리드 모델은 이러한 문제들에 대한 해결책으로 제시됨.

passages.text: During World War II, codebreakers at Bletchley Park faced the daunting Enigma cipher. ( ① ) Polish mathematicians had laid crucial groundwork by reconstructing an early machine. ( ② ) Alan Turing and his colleagues built electromechanical bombes that tested thousands of key settings every minute. ( ③ ) Daily intelligence gleaned from decrypted messages guided Allied convoys around U-boat wolf packs. ( ④ ) Secrecy surrounding the project persisted for decades after the war. ( ⑤ ) Historians continue to debate how much the efforts influenced postwar computing.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Their success shortened the war in Europe by an estimated two years.
questions.explanation: ③에서 암호 해독으로 얻은 정보의 활용 사례를 설명. 주어진 문장은 'Their success'를 받아 이 성공이 전쟁에 미친 거시적인 영향(2년 단축)을 요약. ④ 뒤의 내용은 전후의 비밀 유지에 대해 서술.

passages.text: Residential water use places significant strain on municipal supplies in arid regions. ( ① ) Installing low-flow fixtures is one strategy to reduce consumption. ( ② ) Another involves capturing rainfall in rooftop tanks for later use. ( ③ ) Local codes require separate plumbing to prevent cross-contamination with drinking water. ( ④ ) Homeowners who adopt these measures often see utility bills drop by a third. ( ⑤ ) Community workshops teach residents how to maintain small-scale systems safely.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Greywater from sinks and showers can irrigate gardens after simple filtration.
questions.explanation: ②에서 빗물 저장 탱크를 언급. 주어진 문장은 싱크대와 샤워기에서 나온 물(Greywater)을 재활용하는 또 다른 방법을 제시. ③ 뒤의 'Local codes'는 이러한 재활용 시스템에 필요한 규제를 설명.

passages.text: Bioluminescence—the production of light by living organisms—evolved multiple times across the tree of life. ( ① ) Marine environments host the greatest diversity, from plankton to predators nearly a meter long. ( ② ) The chemical reaction typically involves luciferin substrates and the enzyme luciferase. ( ③ ) Scientists are exploring medical applications such as tracking cancer cells in mice. ( ④ ) Bio-artists have even engineered glowing houseplants using bacterial genes. ( ⑤ ) These experiments raise ethical questions about gene editing for aesthetic purposes.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Fireflies use the glow to identify mates, while deep-sea squid rely on it for camouflage.
questions.explanation: ①에서 생물 발광의 다양성을 언급. 주어진 문장은 그 발광의 '목적'이 종에 따라 다름(짝짓기, 위장)을 예시로 설명. ② 뒤의 내용은 발광의 화학적 원리를 설명.

passages.text: Economists increasingly study social capital alongside traditional indicators like GDP. ( ① ) Trust facilitates transactions by reducing the need for costly contracts and oversight. ( ② ) Comparative studies reveal that regions where neighbors routinely cooperate experience faster growth. ( ③ ) Policy makers therefore invest in programs that encourage civic participation, from volunteering to local referenda. ( ④ ) Measuring intangible qualities such as trust remains methodologically challenging. ( ⑤ ) Critics caution that cultural factors can distort cross-country comparisons.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Nations with higher rates of interpersonal trust tend to attract more foreign investment.
questions.explanation: ②에서 신뢰와 협력이 성장을 촉진함을 설명. 주어진 문장은 이를 국가 단위로 확장하여 '대인 신뢰도'가 외국인 투자 유치에 미치는 영향을 보여줌. ③ 뒤의 정책 입안자들의 노력으로 이어짐.

passages.text: NASA’s Ingenuity helicopter made history as the first powered aircraft on another world. ( ① ) The 1.8-kilogram craft endured freezing nights thanks to a small solar-charged battery. ( ② ) Its carbon-fiber rotors spin five times faster than those of Earth helicopters to compensate for the thin atmosphere. ( ③ ) Early test hops gathered aerial images that complement the Perseverance rover’s ground-level views. ( ④ ) Mission planners will use these photos to chart safer routes and identify science targets. ( ⑤ ) The technology could pave the way for larger aerial scouts on future missions.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Engineers hope later flights will navigate rugged Martian terrain beyond the original landing zone.
questions.explanation: ④에서 수집된 사진의 활용 계획을 설명. 주어진 문장은 'later flights'를 통해 이 헬리콥터의 향후 임무와 희망을 구체적으로 제시. ⑤ 뒤의 내용은 이 기술의 장기적인 비전을 요약.

passages.text: UNESCO’s Intangible Cultural Heritage list includes practices like Korean pansori singing and Mexican Day of the Dead rituals. ( ① ) Nomination requires demonstrating that the tradition is passed down through generations. ( ② ) Communities must also outline plans to safeguard the practice in the future. ( ③ ) Critics argue that formal listing can freeze cultures in time, discouraging natural evolution. ( ④ ) Governments sometimes politicize the process to promote national branding. ( ⑤ ) Scholars call for greater participation by practitioners in decision-making.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Such recognition can revitalize dwindling traditions by attracting funding and tourism.
questions.explanation: ②에서 등재 요건을 설명. 주어진 문장의 'Such recognition'은 유네스코 등재를 가리키며, 그것이 가져오는 긍정적 효과(자금, 관광 유치)를 설명. ③ 뒤의 비판은 이 긍정적 측면과 대조됨.

passages.text: Balancing electricity supply and demand is more complex with intermittent renewables. ( ① ) Demand-response programs encourage consumers to adjust usage when the grid is strained. ( ② ) Smart appliances receive real-time price signals that automate these shifts with minimal inconvenience. ( ③ ) Large industrial facilities participate by pausing non-essential processes for short intervals. ( ④ ) Economists estimate that widespread adoption could negate the need for several peak-load power plants. ( ⑤ ) Privacy advocates, however, worry about utilities collecting detailed household data.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? During heatwaves, the utility offers bill credits to customers who temporarily raise thermostat settings.
questions.explanation: ①에서 수요-반응 프로그램을 소개. 주어진 문장은 폭염 시 요금 할인이라는 구체적인 소비자 참여 예시를 제공. ② 뒤의 스마트 가전은 이 프로그램을 자동화하는 기술을 설명.

passages.text: Children who grow up speaking two languages often outperform monolingual peers on certain cognitive tests. ( ① ) They show particular advantages in delaying gratification and ignoring distractions. ( ② ) Lifelong bilinguals also exhibit a later onset of dementia symptoms on average. ( ③ ) Educators argue that early exposure yields the most pronounced benefits. ( ④ ) Immersion programs therefore introduce second languages as early as kindergarten. ( ⑤ ) Some parents worry about initial confusion, but studies suggest such effects are temporary.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Researchers attribute the effect to strengthened neural pathways involved in task-switching and attention control.
questions.explanation: ①에서 이중 언어 사용 아동의 인지적 이점을 언급. 주어진 문장은 'the effect'를 받아 그 원인이 '신경 경로 강화'에 있다고 설명. ② 뒤의 내용은 또 다른 장기적 이점(치매 지연)을 추가.

passages.text: Commercial aviation is responsible for roughly 2 percent of global CO₂ emissions. ( ① ) Engineers are therefore exploring sustainable aviation fuel made from waste cooking oil and agricultural residues. ( ② ) Several airlines have begun blending small amounts into regular kerosene on selected routes. ( ③ ) Scaling production remains challenging because feedstock supplies are scattered. ( ④ ) Governments are examining tax incentives to attract new biorefineries. ( ⑤ ) Without policy support, analysts fear widespread adoption could take decades.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? That fuel can cut lifecycle carbon emissions by up to 80 percent compared with conventional jet A.
questions.explanation: ①에서 지속가능한 항공유(SAF)를 소개. 주어진 문장의 'That fuel'이 이를 받으며, 탄소 감축 효과를 구체적으로 설명. ② 뒤의 내용은 이 연료의 실제 사용 사례를 제시.

passages.text: Researchers recently dated cave markings in Spain to more than 60,000 years ago—well before modern humans arrived in Europe. ( ① ) The ocher stains line the entrance to a stalactite chamber. ( ② ) Microscopic analysis shows they did not form through natural mineral seepage. ( ③ ) If confirmed, the discovery would make Neanderthals the first known cave artists. ( ④ ) Critics argue that contamination might have skewed the age estimates. ( ⑤ ) Further sampling is planned to settle the debate.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? The pigments appear deliberately mixed, suggesting symbolic intent.
questions.explanation: ②에서 얼룩이 자연 현상이 아님을 밝힘. 주어진 문장은 '의도적으로 혼합된 안료'라는 추가 분석 결과를 제시하며, 이는 ③ 뒤의 '네안데르탈 예술가'라는 가설의 직접적인 근거가 됨.

passages.text: Individual investors often underestimate the power of compound interest. ( ① ) A person who deposits 100 dollars a month at 6 percent annual return will have about 200,000 dollars after 40 years. ( ② ) By contrast, someone who waits ten years to begin must save almost double to catch up. ( ③ ) Financial-literacy courses now use interactive graphs to illustrate the concept. ( ④ ) Policymakers hope better education will narrow retirement-savings gaps. ( ⑤ ) Automatic enrollment in workplace plans is also gaining traction.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? This exponential curve explains why starting early yields disproportionately large gains.
questions.explanation: ②에서 일찍 시작하는 것과 늦게 시작하는 것의 차이를 예시로 보여줌. 주어진 문장은 'This exponential curve'를 통해 이 차이가 발생하는 원리(복리 곡선)를 설명. ③ 뒤의 내용은 이 개념을 교육하는 방법을 설명.

passages.text: Deepfake videos have grown so convincing that politicians worry about disinformation campaigns. ( ① ) Early detection tools focused on irregular blinking patterns, but creators quickly patched that flaw. ( ② ) Researchers are therefore turning to more granular biometric markers. ( ③ ) Initial tests show the method flags forgeries with 94 percent accuracy. ( ④ ) Social-media platforms plan to integrate the technology before major elections. ( ⑤ ) Civil-rights groups insist that transparency about false positives is essential.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Algorithms now examine light reflections in the eyes—details so subtle that human reviewers miss them.
questions.explanation: ②에서 더 세분화된 생체 표식으로 전환한다고 언급. 주어진 문장은 그 구체적인 방법으로 '눈의 빛 반사'를 분석하는 알고리즘을 설명. ③ 뒤의 내용은 이 방법의 초기 테스트 결과를 제시.

passages.text: El Niño refers to the periodic warming of surface waters in the eastern Pacific. ( ① ) The phenomenon disrupts atmospheric circulation on a global scale. ( ② ) Meteorologists use ocean-buoy data and satellite imagery to predict its onset months in advance. ( ③ ) Governments rely on these forecasts to allocate disaster-relief funds. ( ④ ) La Niña, the cooler counterpart, triggers almost opposite weather anomalies. ( ⑤ ) Understanding both cycles remains crucial for climate-adaptation planning.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? During an El Niño year, fisheries off Peru often collapse while floods strike normally arid regions.
questions.explanation: ①에서 엘니뇨가 전 지구적 대기 순환을 방해한다고 설명. 주어진 문장은 그 구체적인 영향으로 페루 어장의 붕괴와 건조 지역의 홍수를 예시로 듦. ② 뒤의 내용은 이러한 현상을 예측하는 방법을 설명.

passages.text: Fermented foods such as kimchi and kefir teem with live bacteria. ( ① ) Dietitians note that regular consumption increases microbial diversity in the gut. ( ② ) A recent clinical trial linked higher diversity to reduced inflammation markers. ( ③ ) Scientists caution, however, that benefits vary depending on individual genetics. ( ④ ) Ultra-processed foods, by contrast, appear to diminish beneficial species. ( ⑤ ) Public-health campaigns now encourage adding at least one fermented item to daily meals.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? These microbes convert fibers into short-chain fatty acids that nourish the intestinal lining.
questions.explanation: ①에서 발효 식품이 장내 미생물 다양성을 높인다고 설명. 주어진 문장은 'These microbes'가 어떻게 장 건강에 기여하는지 그 메커니즘을 설명. ② 뒤의 임상시험은 이 메커니즘의 결과(염증 감소)를 뒷받침.

passages.text: Underground farms use stacked LED racks to grow greens year-round beneath cities. ( ② ) However, cooling costs spike when heat from lamps accumulates, and these bills erase margins. ( ③ ) In these basements, thermostats struggle. ( ④ ) As a result, profit returns. ( ⑤ ) Such freshness sets premium prices.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Heat-exchange ducts now redirect warmth to neighbouring offices, and such symbiosis offsets energy use.
questions.explanation: ③에서 냉각 비용 문제를 제기. 주어진 문장은 '열 교환 덕트'라는 해결책과 그 효과를 설명. ④ 뒤의 'As a result'는 이 해결책으로 인해 수익성이 돌아온다는 결과를 보여줌.

passages.text: Corporate carbon-offset programs plant monoculture pine forests that sequester CO₂ quickly but offer scant habitat diversity. ( ① ) Ecologists advocate mixed-species projects using native seedlings that mature more slowly yet resist pests and wildfire. ( ② ) Accounting frameworks still reward tons captured per dollar, ignoring resilience metrics. ( ③ ) Some companies now purchase “biodiversity credits” alongside carbon certificates, aligning climate and conservation goals. ( ④ ) Sustainable reforestation depends on metrics that value ecological complexity instead of chase short-term numbers. ( ⑤ )

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Regulatory clarity could accelerate this trend by integrating both into one ledger.
questions.explanation: ③에서 기업들이 탄소 배출권과 생물다양성 배출권을 함께 구매하는 추세를 언급. 주어진 문장은 '이러한 추세'를 가속화할 수 있는 정책적 방안(통합 원장)을 제시. ④ 뒤의 내용은 지속가능한 재조림의 조건을 요약.

passages.text: Crowds cheer robotic exoskeleton races where paraplegic athletes sprint down stadium tracks. ( ① ) The spectacle raises awareness, yet most rehab clinics cannot afford six-figure suits. ( ② ) Engineers test 3-D-printed joints that cut costs by two-thirds, but regulatory approvals lag behind innovation. ( ③ ) Charity trials demonstrate life-changing mobility, though battery weight still strains shoulders after extended use. ( ④ ) Insurers remain cautious without long-term durability data. ( ⑤ )

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Closing the accessibility gap will require funding models that scale assistance universally.
questions.explanation: ①~④는 접근성 격차의 여러 측면(비용, 규제, 기술적 한계)을 설명. 주어진 문장은 이 모든 문제를 해결하기 위해 '보편적 지원'을 확장하는 펀딩 모델이 필요하다는 결론을 제시.

passages.text: Coastal cities raise seawalls to block storm surges, yet impermeable barriers reflect wave energy that scours adjacent beaches. ( ① ) Marine architects propose “living breakwaters” built from oyster reefs that grow thicker each season. ( ② ) Pilot sites reduce erosion and revive fisheries, but construction timelines outlast most political cycles. ( ③ ) Voters often demand visible cement before the next election, pushing bureaucrats toward quick fixes. ( ④ ) This highlights a classic conflict between short-term politics and long-term ecological solutions. ( ⑤ )

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Policy continuity will depend on communication campaigns that highlight long-term payoffs.
questions.explanation: ④에서 단기 정치와 장기 생태 해결책 사이의 갈등을 요약. 주어진 문장은 이 갈등을 해결하기 위해 '장기적 이익을 강조'하는 캠페인이 필요하다는 결론적 제안을 함.

passages.text: Augmented-reality glasses overlay repair manuals onto factory machines, slashing downtime for novice technicians. ( ① ) Productivity charts rise, but union leaders worry that skill devaluation will justify staff reductions. ( ② ) Management counters that AR up-skills workers faster, qualifying them for higher-tier tasks. ( ③ ) Economists note that technology often polarizes labor markets unless accompanied by retraining programs. ( ④ ) The debate highlights the dual nature of automation. ( ⑤ )

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Sustainable adoption thus hinges on HR strategies that share efficiency gains rather than erode employment quality.
questions.explanation: ④에서 자동화의 이중적 성격에 대한 논쟁을 요약. 주어진 문장은 '따라서' 지속 가능한 도입은 고용의 질을 잠식하는 대신 '효율성 이익을 공유'하는 전략에 달려있다는 결론을 제시.

passages.text: Invasive lionfish devour juvenile reef fish across the Caribbean, yet their flesh is safe and tasty. ( ① ) Chefs promote “eat the enemy” campaigns, turning ecological menace into gourmet entrée. ( ② ) Fishing tournaments offer cash prizes, but scuba gear limits catch volume compared with the species’ explosive reproduction. ( ③ ) Marine biologists caution that culinary demand must persist past trend cycles to dent populations. ( ④ ) This requires consistent marketing and distribution efforts. ( ⑤ )

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Success will depend on supply chains that keep demand sustained from reef to restaurant.
questions.explanation: ④에서 지속적인 수요의 필요성을 언급. 주어진 문장은 이를 달성하기 위한 구체적인 방법으로 '산지에서 식당까지 이어지는 공급망'을 제시하며 글을 마무리.

passages.text: Digital twins of entire cities run inside supercomputers, testing where floodwaters will collect after record rain. ( ① ) Mayors preview evacuation routes months before clouds gather. ( ② ) Yet unequal data coverage means informal settlements often appear as blank spots, erasing vulnerable populations from simulations. ( ③ ) Civic technologists push for participatory mapping workshops using smartphones and drones. ( ④ ) This approach helps fill the data gaps. ( ⑤ )

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Resilience analytics must therefore include overlooked neighborhoods to be truly effective.
questions.explanation: ④에서 데이터 격차를 메우는 접근법을 설명. 주어진 문장은 '따라서' 진정으로 효과적이려면 '소외된 지역을 포함해야 한다'는 결론을 내림.

passages.text: Gig-economy grocery shoppers race to beat 15-minute delivery promises, weaving scooters through traffic. ( ① ) Insurance premiums skyrocket, but drivers keep phones mounted on handlebars to track constantly updating routes. ( ② ) City officials debate speed-governor mandates, while platforms test batching orders to reduce mileage. ( ③ ) These measures aim to balance speed with safety. ( ④ ) However, the core incentive structure remains unchanged. ( ⑤ )

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Worker safety ultimately rests on algorithms that favor sustainable pacing instead of reward reckless haste.
questions.explanation: ④에서 핵심 인센티브 구조가 변하지 않았다고 지적. 주어진 문장은 '궁극적으로' 근로자 안전은 무모한 속도를 보상하는 대신 '지속 가능한 속도를 선호'하는 알고리즘에 달려있다는 근본적인 해결책을 제시.

passages.text: DNA-editing tools let conservationists contemplate “gene-drive” mosquitoes engineered to resist malaria parasites. ( ① ) Field releases could slash disease burden, yet ecological side-effects remain uncertain. ( ② ) Critics cite past biological controls that backfired, such as cane toads overrunning Australia. ( ③ ) Regulators weigh the moral cost of inaction against potential unintended cascades. ( ④ ) Decision frameworks must balance urgent public health with biodiversity ethics. ( ⑤ )

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Prudent governance will arise from consultations that weigh cross-species risks before deployment.
questions.explanation: ④에서 다양한 이해관계를 저울질해야 하는 의사결정 프레임워크를 언급. 주어진 문장은 신중한 거버넌스는 '배포 전 종간 위험을 평가'하는 협의에서 나올 것이라는 결론을 제시.

passages.text: Remote-sensing satellites detect methane plumes invisible to pilots flying overhead. ( ① ) Oil companies pledge rapid repairs, yet subcontractor crews can take weeks to arrive. ( ② ) A start-up now pairs leak alarms with local drone operators who carry epoxy patches, shrinking response times to hours. ( ③ ) Regulatory credits reward enterprises that meet reduction targets. ( ④ ) Climate advocates warn that monitoring without fines invites green-washing. ( ⑤ )

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Effective mitigation stems from rules that couple accountability measures alongside transparency.
questions.explanation: ④에서 벌금 없는 모니터링의 한계를 지적. 주어진 문장은 효과적인 완화는 투명성과 함께 '책임 조치'를 결합하는 규칙에서 비롯된다는 결론을 제시.

passages.text: Language-learning apps bombard users with daily streak counters and cartoon trophies, funnelling them toward multiple-choice drills. ( ① ) Linguists argue that passive recognition differs from active recall needed for spontaneous conversation. ( ② ) Some platforms now integrate AI chat partners that adapt to learner errors in real time. ( ③ ) Early studies show higher speaking confidence but also highlight over-politeness compared with real-world slang. ( ④ ) This suggests a gap between the app's world and reality. ( ⑤ )

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Pedagogical value depends on dialogue engines that approximate street speech.
questions.explanation: ④에서 앱의 세계와 현실 사이의 격차를 지적. 주어진 문장은 교육적 가치는 '실제 구어에 근접한' 대화 엔진에 달려있다는 결론을 내림.

passages.text: Art forgers have grown more sophisticated with access to industrial pigments and antique canvases. ( ① ) Traditional authentication relies on X-ray fluorescence and provenance documents. ( ② ) Disputes arise when scientific tests contradict family archives. ( ③ ) Museums increasingly enlist computer vision to analyze brush-stroke micro-patterns. ( ④ ) Legal battles can drag on for years, leaving paintings in storage. ( ⑤ ) The stakes are high because a single verdict can swing prices by millions.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Machine-learning experts now train generative adversarial networks to recreate missing portions, aiding curators in spotting inconsistencies.
questions.explanation: ③에서 컴퓨터 비전 분석을 언급. 주어진 문장은 이를 더 발전시킨 '생성적 적대 신경망(GAN)' 기술을 소개. ④ 뒤의 법적 분쟁 내용과 대비됨.

passages.text: Vertical farms stack hydroponic trays inside climate-controlled warehouses. ( ① ) Automation handles seeding, nutrient dosing, and harvesting. ( ② ) Energy remains the largest operating cost, particularly for lighting. ( ③ ) Advocates say proximity to cities slashes food-mile emissions. ( ④ ) Critics question whether the model can scale beyond leafy greens. ( ⑤ ) Government grants support pilots that pair farms with rooftop solar panels.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? LED arrays tuned to specific wavelengths maximize photosynthesis while cutting electricity use.
questions.explanation: ②에서 조명으로 인한 에너지 비용 문제를 제기. 주어진 문장은 '특정 파장에 맞춰진 LED'가 이 문제를 해결하는 구체적인 기술임을 설명. ③ 뒤의 내용은 도시 근접성의 또 다른 이점을 설명.

passages.text: Cognitive-load theory argues that working memory has limited capacity. ( ① ) Overloading students with extraneous information hinders learning. ( ② ) Multimedia presentations that mix flashing text and loud music are prime offenders. ( ③ ) Clear visuals and concise narration lower extraneous demands. ( ④ ) Online course platforms therefore include features like adjustable playback speed. ( ⑤ ) Research shows that spacing content improves long-term retention.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? To reduce load, designers break lessons into short segments followed by low-stakes quizzes.
questions.explanation: ③에서 인지 부하를 줄이는 일반적인 방법을 설명. 주어진 문장은 '부하를 줄이기 위해' 수업을 짧게 나누고 퀴즈를 활용하는 구체적인 설계 전략을 제시. ④ 뒤의 내용은 이러한 전략을 지원하는 플랫폼 기능을 설명.

passages.text: The EU plans to introduce a carbon-border adjustment mechanism in 2026. ( ① ) The policy targets steel, cement, and aluminum initially. ( ② ) Officials fear “carbon leakage” if companies relocate factories to countries with lax rules. ( ③ ) Trading partners warn the tariff could spark retaliation. ( ④ ) Analysts say the measure may incentivize cleaner production abroad. ( ⑤ ) Details of free-allocation phase-outs are still under negotiation.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Importers must purchase certificates matching the embedded emissions of each shipment.
questions.explanation: ①에서 정책 대상을 명시. 주어진 문장은 수입업자가 '어떻게' 이 정책을 준수해야 하는지(인증서 구매) 그 메커니즘을 설명. ② 뒤의 내용은 이 정책의 배경이 되는 '탄소 누출' 우려를 설명.

passages.text: Microcredit is celebrated for expanding access to finance, yet interest charges draw criticism. ( ① ) Traditional banks spread administrative costs over large loans. ( ② ) Micro-lenders, in contrast, visit remote villages to collect weekly payments. ( ③ ) Technology like mobile wallets lowers some operating expenses. ( ④ ) Regulators debate imposing caps that might shrink credit supply. ( ⑤ ) Borrowers’ ability to graduate to formal banking remains an open question.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? High default risk and small loan size explain rates that often exceed 30 percent annually.
questions.explanation: ②에서 소액 대출 기관의 높은 운영 비용을 암시. 주어진 문장은 '높은 채무 불이행 위험과 소액 대출 규모'가 왜 높은 이자율로 이어지는지 그 이유를 명확히 설명. ③ 뒤의 내용은 기술을 통한 비용 절감 노력을 설명.

passages.text: Zero-waste grocery stores are popping up in major cities. ( ① ) The concept aligns with circular-economy principles. ( ② ) Suppliers must adjust logistics to deliver goods in reusable bulk tubs. ( ③ ) Consumer surveys show initial curiosity but concern over convenience. ( ④ ) Some retailers host workshops on DIY cleaners to build community. ( ⑤ ) Mainstream chains experiment with refill stations for detergents.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Customers bring containers from home and pay by weight, eliminating single-use packaging.
questions.explanation: ①에서 제로 웨이스트 상점의 개념을 소개. 주어진 문장은 고객이 '어떻게' 이 시스템에 참여하는지(개인 용기 사용, 무게 단위 결제) 구체적인 방식을 설명. ② 뒤의 내용은 이로 인해 발생하는 공급업체의 변화를 설명.

passages.text: Gravitational microlensing occurs when a foreground star bends light from a more distant object. ( ① ) Unlike transit and radial-velocity methods, it does not require repeated observations. ( ② ) Survey telescopes monitor millions of stars toward the galactic bulge. ( ③ ) Timing the brightening curve reveals the mass of the unseen companion. ( ④ ) Upcoming missions like Roman Space Telescope will expand the catalog. ( ⑤ ) Microlensing is also sensitive to rogue planets unbound to any star.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? The technique has already uncovered a Jupiter-sized planet orbiting a star 25,000 light-years away.
questions.explanation: ③에서 이 기술로 동반 천체의 질량을 밝혀낸다고 설명. 주어진 문장은 'The technique'을 받아 이미 이 기술로 행성을 발견한 구체적인 성공 사례를 제시. ④ 뒤의 내용은 이 성공에 기반한 향후 임무를 설명.

passages.text: Asynchronous programming prevents web apps from freezing during long tasks. ( ① ) Classic Ajax requests rely on event listeners to handle server responses. ( ② ) Nested callbacks, however, lead to code that is hard to maintain—often called “callback hell.” ( ③ ) Modern JavaScript introduces the async/await syntax built on top of promises. ( ④ ) Browser support is nearly universal, reducing the need for polyfills. ( ⑤ ) Debugging tools now visualize pending tasks on the event loop.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Promises make the flow easier to reason about by chaining callbacks in sequence.
questions.explanation: ②에서 '콜백 지옥'이라는 문제를 제기. 주어진 문장은 'Promise'가 이 문제를 해결하는 방법을 설명. ③ 뒤의 내용은 이 Promise를 기반으로 한 더 현대적인 문법(async/await)을 소개.

passages.text: Immune checkpoint inhibitors unleash T cells against cancer. ( ① ) They block proteins like PD-1 that normally act as brakes. ( ② ) Side effects can include autoimmune reactions in skin and gut. ( ③ ) Biomarkers help oncologists select patients most likely to benefit. ( ④ ) Combination therapies with radiation are under clinical trial. ( ⑤ ) High treatment cost remains a barrier in low-income countries.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Yet only tumors with high mutational burden tend to respond robustly to such drugs.
questions.explanation: ③에서 바이오마커를 통해 환자를 선별한다고 설명. 주어진 문장은 'Yet'을 통해, 이러한 약물에 '돌연변이 부담이 높은 종양만' 잘 반응한다는 한계 또는 조건을 추가 설명. ④ 뒤의 내용은 이 한계를 극복하기 위한 병용 요법 연구를 설명.

passages.text: Several nations are piloting central bank digital currencies (CBDCs). ( ① ) Unlike decentralized cryptocurrencies, CBDCs represent a direct claim on the state. ( ② ) Offline-payment functionality is being tested for disaster scenarios. ( ③ ) Programmable features could enable stimulus payments that expire after six months. ( ④ ) Commercial banks worry about deposit flight if citizens hold funds directly with the central bank. ( ⑤ ) International standards for cross-border interoperability are still evolving.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Central banks promise privacy layers, but skeptics fear real-time monitoring of every purchase.
questions.explanation: ③에서 프로그래밍 가능한 기능의 예를 설명. 주어진 문장은 프라이버시에 대한 약속과 감시에 대한 우려라는 상반된 시각을 제시. ④ 뒤의 내용은 상업 은행의 또 다른 우려를 설명.

passages.text: African films are gaining global attention at festivals like Cannes. ( ① ) Directors blend traditional storytelling with modern social issues. ( ② ) Limited budgets force creative solutions such as natural lighting and community actors. ( ③ ) Government incentives vary widely across the continent. ( ④ ) Piracy remains a challenge to revenue generation. ( ⑤ ) Training programs aim to build a skilled technical workforce.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Streaming platforms are funding local projects to meet regional content quotas.
questions.explanation: ③에서 정부 인센티브를 언급. 주어진 문장은 스트리밍 플랫폼의 자금 지원이라는 또 다른 긍정적 요인을 추가. ④ 뒤의 불법 복제 문제는 이러한 긍정적 흐름에 대한 도전 과제를 제시.

passages.text: Electronic transistors face physical limits as sizes shrink below 3 nanometers. ( ① ) Researchers explore optical waveguides etched onto silicon. ( ② ) Integration hurdles include aligning light sources with on-chip modulators. ( ③ ) Hybrid architectures pair photonic cores with traditional processors. ( ④ ) Venture capital is pouring into startups promising AI accelerators. ( ⑤ ) Foundries are racing to adapt lithography tools for new materials.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? By routing electrons as photons, chips could compute at terahertz frequencies with lower heat.
questions.explanation: ①에서 광도파관 연구를 소개. 주어진 문장은 전자를 광자로 라우팅함으로써 얻을 수 있는 이점(속도, 저발열)을 설명. ② 뒤의 내용은 이 기술의 통합 장애물을 설명.

passages.text: Mountain gorillas once hovered near extinction with fewer than 300 individuals. ( ① ) A coalition of governments and NGOs established transboundary parks. ( ② ) Strict guidelines limit group sizes and viewing times. ( ③ ) Local communities receive a share of permit fees to discourage poaching. ( ④ ) Population surveys now count more than 1,000 gorillas. ( ⑤ ) Climate change threatens to shift bamboo flowering seasons, a key food source.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? The resulting tourist revenue funds ranger patrols and veterinary care.
questions.explanation: ③에서 지역 사회에 허가 수수료를 공유한다고 설명. 주어진 문장은 그 '결과로 발생한 관광 수입'이 어떻게 보존 활동에 재투자되는지 구체적으로 설명. ④ 뒤의 내용은 이 모든 노력의 결과인 개체 수 증가를 보여줌.

passages.text: Lithium-sulfur batteries promise triple the energy density of today’s lithium-ion cells. ( ① ) Elemental sulfur is abundant and inexpensive compared with cobalt. ( ② ) Shuttle effects, where polysulfides migrate to the anode, cause rapid capacity fade. ( ③ ) Researchers coat cathodes with porous carbon hosts to trap intermediates. ( ④ ) Automotive companies hope prototypes will meet safety standards within a decade. ( ⑤ ) Recycling pathways remain underdeveloped for sulfur-rich chemistries.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Sulfur’s insulating nature makes fast charging without dendrite formation especially tricky.
questions.explanation: ③에서 셔틀 효과를 막는 방법을 설명. 주어진 문장은 황의 '절연성'이 빠른 충전을 어렵게 만든다는 또 다른 기술적 과제를 제시. ④ 뒤의 내용은 자동차 회사들의 희망을 서술.

passages.text: mRNA vaccines are temperature sensitive during transport. ( ① ) Multi-dose vials complicate inventory management because opened vials expire quickly. ( ② ) Logisticians employ passive containers with phase-change materials. ( ③ ) Real-time data loggers transmit excursions via satellite. ( ④ ) Drones deliver last-mile shipments over rough terrain. ( ⑤ ) Post-pandemic, the cold-chain network could serve other biologics.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Solar-powered fridges in rural clinics keep doses within the required 2-8 °C range.
questions.explanation: ②에서 수동적 보관 용기를 설명. 주어진 문장은 '태양광 냉장고'라는 능동적이고 구체적인 해결책을 제시. ③ 뒤의 실시간 데이터 로거는 또 다른 기술적 보완책.

passages.text: Companies adopt AI résumé screeners to handle large applicant pools. ( ① ) Vendors claim their algorithms reduce human bias. ( ② ) Training data often reflect historical hiring patterns. ( ③ ) Regulators propose requiring impact assessments before deployment. ( ④ ) Open-source toolkits can test models for disparate impact. ( ⑤ ) Some firms revert to random lotteries to ensure fairness.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Audit studies found résumés with identical qualifications but minority-coded names received fewer interview requests.
questions.explanation: ②에서 훈련 데이터가 역사적 편향을 반영한다고 지적. 주어진 문장은 이 편향을 보여주는 구체적인 '감사 연구' 결과를 제시. ③ 뒤의 내용은 이러한 문제에 대한 규제 당국의 대응을 설명.

passages.text: Tourist hotspots in Hawaii and Palau ban certain sunscreens. ( ① ) Mineral filters like non-nano zinc oxide sit on the skin’s surface. ( ② ) Consumers complain of white residue unlike clear chemical lotions. ( ③ ) Cosmetic chemists add tinted iron oxides to improve aesthetics. ( ④ ) Dermatologists stress applying enough product regardless of type. ( ⑤ ) Reef-safe certification labels aim to guide shoppers.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? These formulations omit oxybenzone and octinoxate, chemicals linked to coral bleaching.
questions.explanation: 글의 첫 문장에서 특정 선크림 금지를 언급. 주어진 문장은 'These formulations'를 받아 금지된 선크림이 산호 백화와 관련된 화학물질을 포함하고 있음을 설명. ① 뒤의 내용은 대안인 미네랄 필터를 설명.

passages.text: Classical conditioning underpins much of modern advertising. ( ① ) Pairing a soft-drink logo with upbeat music elicits positive emotions. ( ② ) Repeated exposure forms an association between brand and pleasure. ( ③ ) Regulatory agencies limit ads during children’s TV programming. ( ④ ) Companies now target adults via personalized streaming playlists. ( ⑤ ) Measuring subconscious reactions uses tools like facial EMG and eye-tracking.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Over time, merely hearing the jingle can trigger cravings even without seeing the product.
questions.explanation: ②에서 반복 노출이 연관성을 형성한다고 설명. 주어진 문장은 '시간이 지나면서' 징글만 들어도 갈망을 유발한다는 고전적 조건화의 심화 단계를 설명. ③ 뒤의 내용은 이러한 광고의 규제에 대해 서술.

passages.text: Sinuous stadium roofs and twisting skyscrapers dominate skylines. ( ① ) Algorithms optimize structural strength while minimizing material usage. ( ② ) 3-D printing of formwork accelerates construction of complex shapes. ( ③ ) Building-information models integrate mechanical systems early in the design. ( ④ ) Critics argue such aesthetics can ignore local cultural context. ( ⑤ ) Regulations struggle to keep pace with unconventional geometries.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Parametric tools let architects iterate thousands of options by tweaking a few input curves.
questions.explanation: ①에서 알고리즘 최적화를 설명. 주어진 문장은 '파라메트릭 도구'가 건축가에게 수천 개의 옵션을 반복하게 해준다는 구체적인 설계 과정을 추가 설명. ② 뒤의 내용은 건설 단계의 기술을 설명.

passages.text: Virtual-reality headsets immerse users visually and aurally. ( ① ) Handheld controllers vibrate to signal collisions and gunfire. ( ② ) Researchers develop lightweight gloves with arrays of electroactive pads. ( ③ ) Firmware adjusts feedback based on game physics engines. ( ④ ) Training simulations for surgeons and pilots demand high-fidelity touch cues. ( ⑤ ) Consumer adoption hinges on affordability and hygiene considerations.

questions.text: 주어진 문장이 들어갈 가장 적절한 위치는? Pulses simulate texture, allowing users to feel virtual objects as rough, smooth, or springy.
questions.explanation: ②에서 햅틱 장갑 개발을 언급. 주어진 문장은 '펄스'가 어떻게 질감을 시뮬레이션하는지 그 작동 원리를 설명. ③ 뒤의 펌웨어는 이 피드백을 조정하는 역할을 함.
